# AbstractMusic

> Model-agnostic remote and local music and audio generation abstractions for the Abstract ecosystem.

This file is an AI-readable companion to the repository docs. It summarizes the current public
documentation and points to canonical files for details. It is not the source of truth; the linked
docs and package metadata are canonical.

## Document Index

- [README.md](README.md): overview, installation, quick starts, CLI, text planning, licensing, CI/CD, and Apple/MPS behavior.
- [docs/README.md](docs/README.md): docs tree index.
- [docs/getting-started.md](docs/getting-started.md): install and first-run workflows.
- [docs/repl.md](docs/repl.md): REPL commands, engine/model switching rules, downloads/offline toggle, and duration limits.
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md): core contracts and dependency boundaries.
- [docs/api.md](docs/api.md): public API, request fields, planner hooks, registry, and backend kinds.
- [docs/models.md](docs/models.md): reviewed model list and license/precision policy.
- [docs/faq.md](docs/faq.md): common questions and limits.
- [docs/troubleshooting.md](docs/troubleshooting.md): user-visible failure modes and recovery steps.
- [docs/release.md](docs/release.md): release workflow and publishing setup.
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release history.
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): development and contribution rules.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): private vulnerability reporting.
- [docs/adr/README.md](docs/adr/README.md): architecture decision records index.
- [docs/adr/0001_music_provider_abstraction_and_dependency_policy.md](docs/adr/0001_music_provider_abstraction_and_dependency_policy.md): provider abstraction and dependency policy.
- [docs/backlog/overview.md](docs/backlog/overview.md): current roadmap and lifecycle counts.
- [docs/backlog/README.md](docs/backlog/README.md): backlog layout and operating rules.

---

## README.md

AbstractMusic is a model-agnostic text-to-music/text-to-audio library for the Abstract ecosystem.
The base package is import-light and contains contracts, manager code, CLI/plugin wiring, docs,
model metadata, and stdlib-only ACE Music and ElevenLabs Music remote backends. Heavy local runtime
stacks live behind optional extras such as `acestep`, `musicgen`, `stable-audio`,
`stable-audio-3`, `all-apple`, and `all-gpu`.

The default backend is `acemusic`, which calls a configured hosted API and requires an API key.
The second remote backend is `elevenlabs`, which calls ElevenLabs Music endpoints only and requires
`ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`.
The local ACE-Step route is `acestep`. It uses AbstractMusic-owned orchestration around Diffusers
`AceStepPipeline` and Hugging Face model weights. It does not use an external ACE-Step source tree
or package at runtime.
The `stable-audio-3` route targets `stabilityai/stable-audio-3-small-music` with an
AbstractMusic-owned internal inference subset and Hugging Face weights/configs only. It does not
install or import the upstream Stable Audio runtime package and is currently scoped to
text-to-music. Focused 30-second and 120-second Small Music smokes have passed objective gates, but
broader prompt/seed and GPU validation are still open.

Typical CLI usage:

```bash
abstractmusic t2m "ambient lo-fi study music" --out out.wav --duration 30
abstractmusic --backend elevenlabs t2m "cinematic instrumental synth cue" --format mp3 --out out.mp3 --duration 30
abstractmusic --backend acestep t2m "ambient lo-fi study music" --out out.wav --duration 10
abstractmusic --backend stable-audio-3 t2m "rhythmic space shooter game music" --out out.wav --duration 30 --steps 16
abstractmusic --backend acestep t2m "heroic fantasy epic music" --enhance-prompt --auto-lyrics --print-plan --out out.wav --duration 30
```

For generations of 45 seconds or more, `--structure-prompt` is enabled by default. It can be
disabled with `--no-structure-prompt`.

AbstractMusic separates text planning from audio synthesis. The built-in planner is
dependency-free. Host applications can inject smarter planners through `MusicManager` or plugin
configuration. When hosted by AbstractCore, AbstractMusic may consume a narrow structural text
service exposing `generate_text(...)` or `generate_structured(...)` without importing AbstractCore.
Planner results may include a provider-neutral `composition_plan`; compatible backends such as
`elevenlabs` translate it into native structured music requests.

On Apple hardware, the `acestep` backend tries PyTorch MPS first. The automatic dtype policy
prefers MPS bfloat16 when supported, then MPS float32, with CPU float32 as the final fallback for
invalid audio.

---

## docs/getting-started.md

Install the base package with `pip install abstractmusic`. Use the default remote backend with an
API key, or install a local backend extra before in-process generation. The default local ACE-Step
install profile is:

```bash
pip install "abstractmusic[acestep]"
```

The `stable-audio` extra targets the gated `stabilityai/stable-audio-open-small` checkpoint and
vendors the minimal `stable-audio-tools==0.0.19` model code inside AbstractMusic, so users do not
need to install the upstream `stable-audio-tools` package.
The `stable-audio-3` extra installs only Torch, Transformers, Safetensors, Hugging Face Hub,
NumPy, and Einops for the internal Small Music text-to-music path. It excludes upstream runtime
packages, Flash-Attn, codec libraries, UI, training, and LoRA dependencies.

The public Python entry point is `MusicManager`. Backends are selected explicitly and generation
returns audio bytes or a `GeneratedAsset` depending on the method used. The CLI supports one-shot
generation and an interactive REPL with commands for engine, duration, BPM, seed, lyrics, planner
mode, and model listing.

Fast tests use fakes for most model components. Real model smoke tests are opt-in with
`ABSTRACTMUSIC_RUN_REAL_MODEL_TESTS=1` because checkpoints are large and hardware-sensitive.

---

## docs/repl.md

The REPL (`abstractmusic cli` / `abstractmusic repl`) is the interactive shell for iterating on
prompts, engines, models, and parameters without restarting. It separates **engine** (backend
implementation) from **model** (packaged model id, usually a Hugging Face repo for local engines).

Discovery commands:

- `/engines` lists engines and their default models.
- `/models` lists known packaged models, grouped by engine.
- `/status` shows the active engine, effective model, and key params.

Routing rules:

- `/model <id>` auto-switches the engine when the model is known in the registry.
- Changing `/engine` clears an incompatible explicit `/model` back to the engine default.
- `/download on|off` toggles whether local Hugging Face engines may download missing files.

Stable Audio Open Small (`stable-audio`) is capped at 11 seconds. Stable Audio 3 (`stable-audio-3`)
supports longer durations (Small Music up to 120 seconds; Medium up to 380 seconds).

---

## docs/architecture.md

The core public contract is built around `MusicManager`, `MusicBackend`,
`AudioGenerationRequest`, `GeneratedAsset`, and the model capability registry. Provider-specific
logic belongs in backend modules. Shared fields such as prompt, lyrics, duration, format, seed,
sample rate, and vocal language can be first-class request fields; specialized options should be
explicit and documented.

Text planning is a separate layer. A `MusicPlanningRequest` captures raw user intent and context,
and a `MusicPromptPlan` is compiled into deterministic prompt/lyrics/metadata fields for the
selected backend. AbstractMusic does not import AbstractCore or LLM runtimes for planning.

Heavy imports must remain lazy. The base package stays focused on contracts, manager code,
artifact helpers, docs, CLI/plugin shells, and provider metadata. Runtime engines live behind
optional extras.

---

## docs/api.md

`MusicManager` delegates generation to a configured backend. `generate_audio(...)` returns a
`GeneratedAsset` unless a store is configured; `t2m(...)` returns audio bytes directly.

Planner providers can be callables accepting `request_dict`, objects with
`plan_music_text(request_dict)`, or objects with `create_plan(MusicPlanningRequest)`.
`text_planner_mode="auto"` uses an injected provider when present and falls back to the
deterministic planner. `required` raises on provider failure, and `off` preserves raw text.

The AbstractCore plugin exposes discovery methods such as `available_providers(...)`,
`list_models(...)`, `list_provider_models(...)`, `list_operations(...)`, and
`capability_catalog(...)` from packaged metadata without loading model weights. Discovery uses
backend-oriented provider ids such as `acemusic`, `elevenlabs`, `acestep`, `stable-audio`,
`stable-audio-3`, and `diffusers`, and only returns providers/models whose runtime is usable in the
current environment. It also exposes an optional residency surface (`load_resident_model`,
`list_loaded_models`, `list_resident_models`, `unload_resident_model`) for local backends; remote
providers remain stateless.

Built-in backend kinds include `acemusic`, `elevenlabs`, `acestep`, `diffusers`, `musicgen`,
`stable-audio`, and `stable-audio-3`.

---

## docs/models.md

The model registry lives at `src/abstractmusic/assets/music_model_capabilities.json`. It is
metadata only and must not silently reroute configured providers or models.

Reviewed model entries include:

- `acemusic/ace-step-api`: recommended lightweight remote backend through `acemusic`.
- `elevenlabs/music_v1`: recommended lightweight remote backend through `elevenlabs`, scoped to
  ElevenLabs Music endpoints rather than voice/TTS.
- `ACE-Step/acestep-v15-xl-turbo-diffusers`: recommended local path through `acestep` for local
  generation, MIT, text-to-music with lyrics.
- Additional official ACE-Step checkpoints surfaced under `acestep` are
  `ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5`, `ACE-Step/acestep-v15-base`, and `ACE-Step/acestep-v15-sft`.
- `facebook/musicgen-small`: optional Transformers backend, CC BY-NC 4.0.
- `stabilityai/stable-audio-open-small`: optional gated short-clip backend under the Stability AI
  Community License.
- `stabilityai/stable-audio-3-small-music`: gated Stable Audio 3 Small Music backend through
  `stable-audio-3`, using AbstractMusic-owned runtime code and Hugging Face weights/configs only.
  Focused 30-second and 120-second local smokes have passed, but it is not yet a recommended
  default.
- `stabilityai/stable-audio-3-medium`: tracked behind `stable-audio-3` but not promoted before
  broader Small Music validation.
- Other trained AceStepPipeline-compatible checkpoints may work when selected explicitly, but they
  are not surfaced in discovery until reviewed.
- Additional candidate models are tracked for future optional providers.

The precision rule is to prefer official 8-bit artifacts when available. If none are available,
prefer official 16-bit artifacts.

---

## docs/troubleshooting.md

Common user-visible issues:

- Missing generation packages usually means the backend extra was not installed.
- Missing default remote generation credentials means `ACEMUSIC_API_KEY` is not set. Use
  `ACEMUSIC_BASE_URL` only for compatible custom endpoints.
- If ACE Music returns the wrong duration, use tagged `<prompt>...</prompt>` mode and WAV outputs
  for strict duration enforcement.
- ElevenLabs Music live generation may return HTTP 402 `limited_access` if the key is valid but
  the account tier does not include Music API access.
- ACE-Step model access uses Hugging Face model weights and the standard Hugging Face cache, not
  local ACE-Step source-tree paths.
- Apple MPS can fall back to CPU when generation returns non-finite audio; forcing CPU is supported
  with `--device cpu`.
- Long generations may need explicit structure, seed changes, or clearer prompts to avoid static or
  repetitive output.
- Stable Audio Open Small requires Hugging Face gate approval and a token exposed as `HF_TOKEN` or
  `HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN`.
- Stable Audio 3 Small Music requires Hugging Face gate approval and a token exposed as `HF_TOKEN`
  or `HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN`; do not install an upstream Stable Audio 3 runtime package.
- The release workflow refuses duplicate PyPI versions and requires manual publish confirmation
  when dispatched by hand.

---

## docs/release.md

The canonical version source is `src/abstractmusic/_version.py`; `pyproject.toml` reads it
dynamically. Tags use `v<version>`.

Local preflight includes:

```bash
python -m pytest -q -m unit
python -m build
python -m twine check dist/*
mkdocs build --strict
```

Manual release dispatch defaults to `publish=false` for rehearsal. Publishing requires
`publish=true` and `publish_confirmation=publish-abstractmusic-<version>`. PyPI trusted
publishing uses the `pypi` environment, and documentation deploys through GitHub Pages using the
`github-pages` environment.

---

## CHANGELOG.md

The changelog records user-visible changes only. Version `0.1.5` adds the stdlib-only ElevenLabs
Music backend, the internal Stable Audio 3 Small Music backend, and the provider-neutral
`MusicCompositionPlan` path. It keeps Stable Audio 3 behind a minimal optional extra and excludes
upstream Stable Audio runtime packages, Flash-Attn, codec libraries, UI, training, LoRA, and local
checkout paths from that profile.

---

## CONTRIBUTING.md

Contributors should keep `MusicManager` model-agnostic, keep heavy imports lazy, avoid committing
model weights or generated audio, update model capability metadata for provider changes, and add
clear errors for unsupported request fields. Backend additions should include metadata, tests,
docs for dependencies/hardware/license/precision, and real smoke tests when practical.

---

## SECURITY.md

Security reports should be sent privately to `contact@abstractcore.ai`. Reports should include
impact, reproduction steps, affected versions or commits, relevant logs/configuration, and
suggested mitigation when available.

---

## docs/adr/README.md

# Architecture Decision Records

ADRs record decisions that shape multiple backlog items or public APIs.

Use ADRs for provider abstraction rules, dependency-profile policy, model/license policy, fallback
policy, and request/result schema decisions. Keep each ADR short and actionable, and link it from
the backlog items it governs.

---

## docs/adr/0001_music_provider_abstraction_and_dependency_policy.md

# ADR 0001: Music Provider Abstraction And Dependency Policy

## Status

Accepted

## Context

AbstractMusic must expose a unified way to generate music while supporting multiple local and
remote-capable model families. Music generation models are heavy, hardware-sensitive, and unevenly
licensed. A default install that eagerly pulls every local runtime would conflict with the
AbstractCore and AbstractVision direction.

## Decision

AbstractMusic will keep a stable public request/result abstraction above provider-specific
backends. Provider-specific features such as lyrics, vocal language, duration parameter names,
guidance behavior, reference audio, or video input must be expressed through explicit capability
metadata and first-class request fields where they are broadly useful.

Heavy local inference stacks must be lazy-imported and should live behind optional extras once the
packaging split is implemented. Default providers should prefer permissive, commercially usable
licenses. Non-commercial or license-unclear providers may exist only as explicit optional providers
with clear warnings and tests that prove they cannot be selected silently as defaults.

## Consequences

- The manager stays thin and model-agnostic.
- Backends must expose enough capability metadata for callers and AbstractCore to avoid accidental
  unsupported calls.
- ACE-Step can be the primary local provider, but the public API cannot become ACE-Step-shaped.
- TinyMozart and Omni2Sound are not default providers under this policy.

## Enforcement

- Backlog items that add providers must update capability metadata and docs.
- Provider tests must cover unsupported parameter handling instead of silently ignoring inputs.
- Packaging tests should verify dependency profiles after the local-extra split.

---

## docs/backlog/overview.md

# AbstractMusic Backlog Overview

## Current status

AbstractMusic has a thin public manager, a minimal backend protocol, stdlib-only ACE Music and
ElevenLabs Music remote backends, a generic Diffusers audio backend, a single public ACE-Step
backend built on `AceStepPipeline`, an internal Stable Audio 3 Small Music spike, and an
AbstractCore capability plugin. The ACE-Step path must not call a local ACE-Step source checkout or
external ACE-Step package, and the Stable Audio 3 path must not import or wrap the upstream Stable
Audio runtime package.

The repository has the baseline docs, hygiene files, import-light packaging, a model registry, a
CLI REPL, and a real smoke validation harness. Previous external-runtime experiments are no longer
supported provider paths; accepted reference WAVs remain comparison artifacts only.

## Status counts

- Planned: 2
- Proposed: 2
- Completed: 12
- Deprecated: 5
- Recurrent: 2

## Priority bands

- P1: keep provider routing, defaults, and duration handling truthful and robust.
- P1: broaden local-engine validation (Stable Audio 3 and ACE-Step Diffusers) before changing any
  recommendation/default status.
- P2: evaluate optional/non-core model families without polluting the base install.

## Next recommended work

1. Complete `planned/055_heartmula_optional_provider.md`.
2. Complete `planned/060_yue_optional_provider.md`.

## Planned work

| Priority | Item | Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| P2 | `planned/055_heartmula_optional_provider.md` | Evaluate and implement HeartMuLa as an optional lyrics/tags music provider if dependency and runtime boundaries are acceptable. |
| P2 | `planned/060_yue_optional_provider.md` | Evaluate YuE as an optional multi-stage lyrics-to-music provider without claiming partial token generation is audio. |

## Completed work

| Completed | Item | Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2026-05-15 | `completed/000_critical_assessment_and_roadmap.md` | Preserved the assessment and created the implementation roadmap. |
| 2026-05-15 | `completed/010_repo_hygiene_docs_and_packaging.md` | Added repo/docs baseline, license/security/contribution files, ignore rules, and cleaned generated tracked artifacts. |
| 2026-05-15 | `completed/020_music_abstraction_and_capability_registry.md` | Added capability types, request fields, backend capability hooks, packaged model registry, and registry tests. |
| 2026-05-15 | `completed/040_real_generation_validation_matrix.md` | Added opt-in real generation tests and tightened WAV/music-likeness inspection after a short MPS smoke failed listening review. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/030_acestep_diffusers_xl_provider.md` | Added and validated the package-owned ACE-Step XL Turbo path that now backs the public `acestep` backend. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/045_audio_artifact_screening_and_quality_metadata.md` | Strengthened artifact screening and made validation state more explicit through smoke metrics, tests, and registry status. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/050_dependency_profiles_and_optional_providers.md` | Added the lightweight ACE Music remote backend, kept base dependencies empty, expanded local platform extras, and documented optional provider boundaries. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/065_acestep_repetition_quality_gate.md` | Added repetition/novelty-oriented artifact screening and wired it into ACE-Step quality reporting. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/0083_stable_audio_3_local_provider_spike.md` | Implemented and validated Stable Audio 3 Small Music as a package-owned local backend (Small validated; Medium deferred). |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/0084_music_capability_residency_contract.md` | Exposed Core-friendly load/list/unload residency for local music engines without confusing remote discovery with local loaded state. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/0085_truthful_stable_audio_capability_registration_and_music_routing.md` | Registered Stable Audio Open Small as a real capability backend and aligned discovery/catalog routing truth. |
| 2026-05-21 | `completed/0086_repl_ux_and_default_model_routing_hardening.md` | Made the REPL discoverable and robust (engine/model routing, aligned model listing, downloads toggle, and data-driven defaults). |

## Proposed work

| Item | Promotion criteria |
| --- | --- |
| `proposed/0080_text_planning_provider_contract_for_music.md` | Promote when advanced music quality requires LLM-generated captions/lyrics/metadata and a no-AbstractCore-dependency planner boundary is clear. |
| `proposed/0082_local_engine_priority_after_remote_baseline.md` | Promote when choosing the next local/open-weight engine spike after the two remote endpoint baseline. |

## Deprecated work

| Deprecated | Item | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2026-05-20 | `deprecated/0025_external_acestep_runtime_wrapper.md` | Removed the out-of-package ACE-Step runtime wrapper path; standalone package code is required. |
| 2026-05-21 | `deprecated/035_acestep_v15_backend_compatibility_hardening.md` | Superseded by the single-backend `acestep` contract; the old standalone `acestep-v15` path is no longer part of the supported surface. |
| 2026-05-21 | `deprecated/0081_music_install_profile_boundary.md` | Superseded by completed dependency-profile implementation. |
| 2026-05-21 | `deprecated/0075_stable_audio_open_small_validation.md` | Open Small validation is no longer tracked as separate planned work; keep it as an optional legacy backend and focus validation on Stable Audio 3. |
| 2026-05-21 | `deprecated/070_musicgen_small_optional_provider.md` | MusicGen Small remains an optional non-commercial backend, but we are not pursuing further validation/recommendation work as part of the core roadmap. |

## Completion process

When a planned item is complete:

1. Add a completion report to the item.
2. Set `Status: Completed` and `Completed: <YYYY-MM-DD>`.
3. Move it from `planned/` to `completed/`.
4. Update this overview, including counts and any changed priorities.
5. Run recurrent tasks whose conditions apply.

## Planning notes

- ACE-Step discovery/UI must stay on the single public `acestep` backend contract.
- Previous external-runtime smoke artifacts remain useful as references, but they do not define the
  supported package path.
- On Apple hardware, prefer PyTorch MPS for the `acestep` backend with clear CPU fallbacks for
  known unstable decode steps.
- No additional model should be marked recommended until it passes a real generation smoke test
  through AbstractMusic.
- The ACE-Step XL Turbo Diffusers checkpoint is the cleanest near-term improvement path
  because it uses a standard Diffusers pipeline layout and `AceStepPipeline`.
- `facebook/musicgen-small` is a useful small validation provider because it has a simple
  Transformers path and 300M weights, but it is CC BY-NC 4.0 and must not become the commercial
  default.
- `stabilityai/stable-audio-open-small` is a 341M gated short text-to-audio model worth tracking
  for sound effects/loops and Apple/Arm-friendly experiments, but it is not currently a strong
  default music model candidate.
- TinyMozart is small and local but is unconditional MIDI piano, not general prompt-to-music, and
  has no declared license in the Hugging Face metadata reviewed on 2026-05-15.
- Omni2Sound is useful research for multimodal audio/foley, but it is CC BY-NC 4.0, CUDA/script
  oriented, very large, and not suitable for a default commercial-capable music provider.
- HeartMuLa can generate lyrics/tags conditioned music according to its Apache-2.0 Hugging Face
  model card and official `heartlib` deployment docs. It should be evaluated as an optional
  provider because it requires HeartCodec and a heavy runtime stack.
- YuE Stage-1 is music-relevant and Apache-2.0, but it is not an end-to-end audio generator by
  itself. A YuE provider needs the Stage-2 and codec/upsampler path before it can satisfy
  AbstractMusic's generation goal.
- With ACE Music and ElevenLabs Music in the base package, remote coverage is sufficient for now.
  Future provider work should prioritize local/open-weight generation unless a remote endpoint
  proves a reusable abstraction needed by local engines.
- MiniMax documents a `music-2.6-free` API model, but its pricing and access pages still frame
  music usage around token plans, pay-as-you-go balance, and limited-free quotas. Do not add it as
  a third remote backend unless a user explicitly wants that provider and accepts the access
  ambiguity.
- Stable Audio 3.0 Small now has an internal package-owned text-to-music path that passed focused
  30-second and 120-second validation runs at 16 steps. It is still a spike, not a recommended
  default, until broader prompt/seed and GPU validation are recorded.
- Stable Audio Open Small is registered and routed truthfully through the AbstractCore plugin as
  `abstractmusic:stable-audio`, but it remains a gated short-clip legacy backend and is not marked
  recommended.

---

## docs/backlog/README.md

# AbstractMusic Backlog

This folder is the engineering memory for AbstractMusic.

The backlog is intentionally code-first: before implementing or changing an item,
inspect the current code and docs because this project is moving quickly and older
notes may be stale.

## Layout

- `overview.md`: status, priority order, and work ledger.
- `planned/`: committed work that should be implemented.
- `proposed/`: useful ideas that need more evidence before implementation.
- `completed/`: finished work with completion reports.
- `deprecated/`: retired work with deprecation reports.
- `recurrent/`: checklist tasks that should run after relevant changes.

## Rules

- Keep planned items standalone.
- Preserve the model/provider abstraction; do not hard-code one model into the public API.
- Keep heavy local inference dependencies optional where possible.
- Do not commit model weights, generated WAVs, Python bytecode, or cache artifacts.
- Prefer permissive, commercially usable model licenses for default providers.
- Never silently ignore generation inputs such as lyrics, duration, seed, model choice, fallback, or truncation.
- Validate with fast unit tests and at least one real model smoke path before calling provider work complete.
